provenance.source_corpus: Understory Succession following Initial Ecosystem Restoration Treatments in Ingrown Dry Forests

source_spans.text: The restoration treatment consisted of partial-cutting the stands to thin the forest canopy and remove intermediate layer trees.
Understory light increased by 30% following the treatment.
The general response of understory vegetation was a decline in production and cover followed by recovery to pre-treatment levels.
In fact, exposed soil increased from 0.8% to 6% following harvesting.

nodes.name: Occurred damage and mortality of plants

source_spans.text: The ground-based harvesting equipment, no doubt, caused damage and mortality of plants at the site.
Fire suppression, overgrazing, and selective logging in these forests are believed to have caused forest encroachment on grasslands and ingrowth within open forests.
Fire suppression, overgrazing, and selective logging in these forests are believed to have caused forest encroachment on grasslands and ingrowth within open forests.
Ingrowth often results in the retrogression of understory plant communities, the loss of habitat for sensitive wildlife species, and an increased risk of catastrophic wildfires.
